title: 10 best herbal tinctures for dry skin
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for dry skin are concentrated liquid extracts of plants, created by soaking plant material in a solvent such as alcohol or glycerin. These tinctures can help alleviate dry skin by providing hydration and nourishment, while also protecting the skin's natural barrier. Examples of herbal tinctures beneficial for dry skin include chamomile, calendula, and licorice root. Chamomile tincture has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe irritation and redness, while calendula tincture promotes skin repair and regeneration. Licorice root tincture has been shown to improve skin hydration and elasticity. By incorporating these herbal tinctures into skincare routines, individuals with dry skin can experience improved skin health, reduced irritation, and enhanced overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tincture helps with dry skin because it contains anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties. The tincture is made from the flowers and leaves of the yarrow plant, which are rich in azulene, a compound known for its soothing effects on irritated skin. Yarrow tincture also contains tannins, which have astringent properties that help to tighten and tone the skin, reducing the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles. Additionally, yarrow tincture is a natural emollient, helping to lock in moisture and keep the skin hydrated. This makes it an ideal natural remedy for dry, flaky, and itchy skin, providing relief and comfort.
remedy_properties: Astringent: Yarrow tincture's astringent property tightens and tones skin, reducing dryness and flakiness, due to its tannin content.
Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tincture's anti-inflammatory property soothes irritated dry skin, reducing redness and inflammation, due to its lactone content.
Antimicrobial: Yarrow tincture's antimicrobial property protects dry skin from infection, promoting skin health, due to its sesquiterpene lactone content.
Emollient: Yarrow tincture's emollient property softens and moisturizes dry skin, improving skin texture, due to its volatile oil content.
Circulation-stimulating: Yarrow tincture's circulation-stimulating property increases blood flow to dry skin, promoting nutrient and oxygen supply, due to its azulene content.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in anti-inflammatory properties, yarrow flowers help soothe irritated, dry skin.
Leaves: Containing tannins and flavonoids, yarrow leaves can help reduce redness and alleviate dryness.
Seeds: Packed with essential fatty acids, yarrow seeds help nourish and moisturize dry, flaky skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried yarrow leaves stems and flowers in mid-summer morning.
Cleanly chop 1 part yarrow herb with 2 parts vodka or brandy in a glass jar.
Store the mixture at room temperature for 4 to 6 weeks allowing for maceration.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar.
Label and store the final tincture in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tincture helps with dry skin because it contains mucilage, a substance that forms a protective layer on the skin, helping to lock in moisture and soothe irritation. Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, marshmallow tincture helps to reduce redness, inflammation, and irritation associated with dry skin. Its emollient properties help to soften and smooth the skin, while its hydrating effects promote the restoration of the skin's natural barrier function. By nourishing and moisturizing the skin, marshmallow tincture helps to alleviate dryness, leaving the skin feeling soft, supple, and rejuvenated.
remedy_properties: Demulcent property: Marshmallow tincture contains mucilage that forms a protective layer on the skin, helping to lock in moisture and soothe dry, irritated skin.
Emollient property: The high mucilage content in marshmallow tincture provides a soothing and smoothing effect on the skin, making it feel soft and supple.
Anti-inflammatory property: The flavonoids and triterpene saponins present in marshmallow tincture help reduce inflammation and redness associated with dry skin.
Hydrating property: Marshmallow tincture's mucilage has the ability to absorb and retain water, providing long-lasting hydration to dry, flaky skin.
Wound-healing property: The mucilage and polysaccharides in marshmallow tincture promote tissue repair and wound healing, making it an effective treatment for dry, cracked skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: The marshmallow root contains mucilage, which helps to soothe and hydrate dry skin.
Leaves: The leaves of the marshmallow plant are rich in flavonoids and polyphenols, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help relieve dry, itchy skin.
Flowers: Marshmallow flowers have a high mucilage content, making them effective in treating dry, rough skin.
Seeds: The seeds of the marshmallow plant contain pectin and polysaccharides, which form a protective layer on the skin, helping to retain moisture and alleviate dryness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest althaea officinalis roots dig deeply into the soil to obtain 2-3 pounds of clean fresh material.
Dry the harvested roots spread them out in a single layer on paper or screens for 7-10 days.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces place them in a coffee grinder and process until reduced to fine powder.
Steep the powdered roots in vodka at a ratio of 1 part herb to 2 parts solvent for 14 days stirring daily.
Strain and filter the tincture use a cheesecloth or coffee filter to remove plant material from liquid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock tincture helps with dry skin because it contains anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties that soothe and hydrate the skin. Burdock is rich in fatty acids, essential minerals, and vitamins that nourish and protect the skin's barrier function, preventing moisture loss. Its powerful antioxidants help to scavenge free radicals that cause skin damage, promoting healthy skin cell regeneration. Burdock tincture also has detoxifying properties that help to eliminate toxins from the body, promoting overall skin health. All these benefits make burdock tincture an effective natural remedy for dry and damaged skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Burdock tincture helps reduce dry skin inflammation, soothing irritation and redness, due to the presence of essential fatty acids and tannins.
Moisturizing: Burdock tincture provides hydration to dry skin by containing mucilage, a substance that forms a protective layer on the skin, trapping moisture.
Antioxidant: Burdock tincture helps protect dry skin from environmental stressors by containing phenolic acids and flavonoids, which neutralize free radicals.

Detoxifying: Burdock tincture supports skin health by promoting the removal of toxins and waste products from the body, which can contribute to dry skin issues, due to its diuretic and blood purifying properties.
Wound healing: Burdock tincture promotes skin regeneration and repair, helping to alleviate dry skin conditions, thanks to its content of essential fatty acids, vitamins, and minerals.
remedy_parts: Roots: Burdock roots contain essential fatty acids and phytosterols that help to soothe and moisturize dry skin.
Leaves: Rich in vitamins and minerals, burdock leaves can help to nourish and hydrate dry, flaky skin.
Seeds: Burdock seeds are a great source of polyunsaturated fatty acids, which can help to restore the skin's natural barrier function and lock in moisture.
Flowers: Burdock flowers contain flavonoids and tannins that have astringent properties, which can help to tighten and tone dry, aging skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried burdock roots in late summer or early fall weighing approximately 10 ounces.
Clean and chop the burdock roots into small pieces to ensure optimal extraction of bioactive compounds.
Combine the chopped burdock roots with high-proof vodka or ethanol 75-80% at a ratio of one part roots to three parts solvent.
Transfer the mixture to a clean glass jar and store it in a cool dark place for six to eight weeks agitating weekly.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids and store in refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tincture helps with dry skin because it contains anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties. The herb's active compounds, such as flavonoids and carotenoids, help to soothe irritation and reduce redness. Moreover, pot marigold tincture creates a barrier on the skin, preventing moisture loss and keeping the skin hydrated. This herbal remedy also promotes skin cell regeneration, which helps to improve the skin's overall texture and appearance. Therefore, pot marigold tincture is an effective natural treatment for dry and damaged skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Pot marigold tincture helps reduce inflammation, which is important for dry skin as it soothes irritation and redness. This property is attributed to the presence of anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and carotenoids.

Emollient property: Pot marigold tincture has a soothing effect on the skin, making it supple and moisturized. This is due to the emollient properties of its active constituents, such as triterpenoids and polyacetylenes.

Antimicrobial property: Pot marigold tincture has antimicrobial properties that help protect the skin from infection. This is important for dry skin as it can be more prone to irritation and infection. The antimicrobial properties are attributed to the presence of compounds like flavonoids and essential oils.

Wound-healing property: Pot marigold tincture promotes wound healing, which is important for dry skin as it can help repair damaged skin. This property is due to the presence of active constituents like flavonoids, volatile oils, and carotenoids, which promote tissue regeneration.

Antioxidant property: Pot marigold tincture has antioxidant properties that help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. This is important for dry skin as it can help prevent further damage and keep the skin healthy. The antioxidant properties are attributed to the presence of active constituents like flavonoids, carotenoids, and phenolic acids.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Pot marigold flowers are rich in flavonoids and carotenoids, making them effective in soothing dry and irritated skin.
Leaves: The leaves of the pot marigold plant contain high levels of antioxidants and are often used in tinctures to treat dry and damaged skin.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds are a great source of essential fatty acids and are often used in skincare products for their moisturizing properties.
Roots: Although less commonly used, the roots of the pot marigold plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds and are sometimes included in tinctures for dry skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers on a sunny day when petals are fully open.
Dry the harvested flowers using a food dehydrator or air-drying method for 24 hours.
Combine 1 cup of dried calendula officinalis flowers with 2 cups of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place and shake it gently every day for 6 weeks to extract the active compounds.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Roman chamomile tincture helps with dry skin because it has anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties. It contains active compounds that soothe irritation and reduce redness, helping to alleviate the discomfort associated with dry skin. The tincture also helps to lock in moisture, promoting hydration and softness. Its antioxidant properties further protect the skin from damage caused by environmental stressors, helping to maintain the skin's health and vitality. Regular use of roman chamomile tincture can help restore the skin's natural balance, leaving it feeling smooth, supple, and nourished.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Roman chamomile tincture helps reduce dry skin inflammation, soothing irritation and redness, due to its active constituents like azulene and chamazulene.
Moisturizing: It provides hydration to the skin, alleviating dryness, thanks to the presence of essential fatty acids and antioxidants.
Wound healing: Roman chamomile tincture promotes skin cell regeneration and tissue repair, addressing dry skin issues, owing to its flavonoids and terpenoids content.
Antimicrobial: It inhibits the growth of harmful bacteria and fungi, preventing dry skin infections, attributable to its active components such as bisabolol and matricin.
Soothing: Roman chamomile tincture calms and relaxes the skin, providing relief from itching and discomfort, due to its calming properties, which include apigenin and luteolin.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in azulene, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe irritated and dry skin.
Leaves: Contain flavonoids and essential oils that have a calming effect on the skin, helping to reduce redness and dryness.
Buds: High in antioxidants, buds help protect the skin from environmental stressors, while providing moisture and nourishment to dry skin.
Stems: Contain therapeutic compounds that have astringent properties, helping to tighten and tone dry skin, reducing the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried roman chamomile flowers at full bloom for maximum potency and quality.
Clean and chop the harvested herbs into small pieces to increase their surface area for extraction.
Combine one part chopped roman chamomile with three parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring a consistent ratio of 1:3.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for four weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate efficient extraction and prevent mold growth.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids and preserving the clear tincture solution for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chicory tincture helps with dry skin because it contains anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties that soothe and hydrate the skin. Chicory is rich in betulinic acid, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and improve skin hydration. Additionally, chicory contains mucilage, a substance that forms a protective layer on the skin, helping to lock in moisture and protect the skin from environmental stressors. Regular use of chicory tincture can help to improve the skin's overall texture and tone, leaving it feeling soft, supple, and hydrated.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chicory tincture's anti-inflammatory property is important for dry skin because it helps reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with dry skin conditions. This property is attributed to the presence of anti-inflammatory compounds like sesquiterpenes and polyphenols.

Demulcent property: Chicory tincture's demulcent property is important for dry skin because it forms a soothing, protective film over the skin, helping to lock in moisture and reduce dryness. The mucilaginous constituents, such as inulin and fructans, contribute to this demulcent effect.

Emollient property: Chicory tincture's emollient property is important for dry skin because it helps soften and smooth the skin, making it more flexible and less prone to cracking. The emollient action is largely due to the presence of fatty acids and waxes found in chicory.

Antioxidant property: Chicory tincture's antioxidant property is important for dry skin because it helps protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals. The antioxidant activity is attributed to the presence of phenolic compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, found in chicory.

Wound-healing property: Chicory tincture's wound-healing property is important for dry skin because it promotes skin regeneration and tissue repair, helping to improve the overall appearance and health of dry, damaged skin. This property is attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds, including tannins, polyphenols, and flavonoids, that contribute to the wound-healing process.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain high levels of inulin, which helps to lock in moisture and soothe dry, irritated skin.
Leaves: Rich in vitamins and minerals, they can help to nourish and hydrate dry skin.
Flowers: Contain antioxidants that can help to protect the skin from damage and support skin cell regeneration, reducing the appearance of dry, flaky skin.
Seeds: High in essential fatty acids, they can help to restore the skin's natural barrier function and lock in moisture.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality cichorium intybus roots and chop them into small pieces weighing approximately 10% of the total vodka volume.
Combine chopped roots with 70% vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring that the plant material is completely submerged.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks to allow for maceration and extraction of bioactive compounds.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid extract.
Transfer the filtered tincture to dark glass dropper bottles and store them in the refrigerator to slow down oxidation and preserve potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slicing cucumber tincture helps with dry skin because it contains high levels of vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants that are beneficial for skin health. Cucumbers are naturally hydrating, and the tincture can help replenish moisture in the skin. Additionally, cucumber extract has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe irritation and reduce redness. The herbal preparation can also improve skin elasticity and tone, giving it a healthier and more youthful appearance. Overall, slicing cucumber tincture is a natural and effective remedy for dry skin.
remedy_properties: Moisturizing: Slicing cucumber tincture contains high levels of water and triterpenes, which provide intense hydration and help to soothe dry, itchy skin.
Anti-inflammatory: The presence of flavonoids and other antioxidants in cucumber tincture helps reduce inflammation, soothing irritated and flaky skin.
Antioxidant: Rich in vitamins C and E, cucumber tincture provides potent antioxidant properties, protecting skin from free radical damage and promoting skin cell regeneration.
Astringent: Cucumber tincture contains tannins, which have a mild astringent effect, helping to tighten and tone dry, rough skin.
Soothing: The cooling and calming properties of cucumber tincture, due to its high water and mineral content, help alleviate dry skin discomfort and promote overall skin health.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Slicing cucumber seeds contain high levels of fatty acids and nutrients that help to nourish and moisturize dry skin, making them ideal for medicinal tinctures.
Leaves: The leaves of the slicing cucumber plant are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which can help to soothe and heal dry, irritated skin.
Flowers: Cucumber flowers are known for their cooling and astringent properties, making them useful for treating dry skin and reducing the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles.
Roots: The roots of the slicing cucumber plant contain a variety of beneficial compounds, including saponins and flavonoids, which can help to hydrate and protect dry skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cucumbers at peak ripeness for optimal flavor and nutrient content.
Clean the cucumbers thoroughly by washing them in cold water with mild soap.
Slice the cucumbers thinly to increase surface area for efficient extraction.
Combine sliced cucumbers with 80-proof vodka or other high-proof solvent in a glass jar at ratio of 1:2 (wet to dry).
Store the mixture in a cool dark place and let it steep for 6-8 weeks before straining and bottling.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild carrot tincture helps with dry skin because it contains high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb's seeds, roots, and leaves are rich in vitamins A, C, and E, which are essential for maintaining healthy skin. These vitamins help to moisturize and soothe dry, irritated skin, while also promoting the production of collagen, a protein that keeps skin firm and elastic. Moreover, wild carrot tincture has antiseptic and astringent properties that help to cleanse and tighten pores, reducing the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles. Regular use of wild carrot tincture can help to restore the skin's natural moisture balance, leaving it soft, supple, and hydrated.
remedy_properties: Emollient: Wild carrot tincture contains moisturizing properties that help soothe and smooth dry skin, thanks to its high content of polyunsaturated fatty acids.
Anti-inflammatory: The tincture's anti-inflammatory properties, derived from its active constituents like flavonoids and carotenoids, help reduce redness and irritation associated with dry skin.
Antioxidant: Wild carrot tincture's antioxidant properties, attributed to its rich supply of vitamin A, C, and E, protect the skin from environmental stressors and help maintain skin hydration.
Circulation-boosting: Wild carrot tincture contains active constituents like vitamin C and K that promote blood flow, which aids in delivering essential nutrients and moisture to dry skin.
Exfoliating: The tincture's exfoliating properties, due to its natural content of essential oils, help remove dead skin cells, allowing for better absorption of moisture and improved skin texture.
remedy_parts: Roots: Wild carrot roots contain high levels of moisture and nutrients that can help soothe and hydrate dry skin.
Leaves: The leaves of wild carrot plants have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and irritation associated with dry skin.
Flowers: Wild carrot flowers contain antioxidants that can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate dry skin.
Seeds: Wild carrot seeds are rich in essential fatty acids that can help nourish and moisturize dry skin, while also promoting skin elasticity.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried wild carrot roots and leaves, ensuring proper identification and quality control.
Chop the harvested material into small pieces, using scissors or a knife for precise cutting.
Combine the chopped plant material with 40% vodka or everclear in a clean glass jar, filling the jar to within an inch of the top.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place and allow it to macerate for 2-6 weeks, shaking daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and preserving the liquid extract.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horsetail tincture helps with dry skin because it contains high levels of silica and antioxidants. Silica is a mineral that promotes collagen production, which can improve skin elasticity and hydration. Antioxidants found in horsetail tincture, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals and environmental stressors. These compounds work together to improve skin moisture, reduce inflammation, and promote overall skin health. Additionally, horsetail tincture has astringent properties that can help tighten and tone the skin, further reducing the appearance of dryness and roughness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Horsetail tincture's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce redness and irritation associated with dry skin, thanks to its silica, saponins, and flavonoids content.
Moisturizing: Horsetail tincture's moisturizing property helps hydrate dry skin, as it contains silica and antioxidants that support skin elasticity and cell regeneration.
Astringent: Horsetail tincture's astringent property helps tighten skin and reduce the appearance of fine lines, due to its tannins and silica content, which strengthen skin cells.
Antioxidant: Horsetail tincture's antioxidant property helps protect dry skin from environmental stressors, thanks to its phenolic acids and flavonoids content, which combat free radicals.

Collagen synthesis: Horsetail tincture's ability to stimulate collagen synthesis helps improve skin texture and suppleness, due to its silica and saponins content, which promote skin health and regeneration.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Horsetail rhizomes are rich in silica and other minerals, making them useful for creating tinctures that help improve skin elasticity and reduce dryness.
Stems: Horsetail stems contain bioactive compounds that can help to tone and tighten skin, reducing the appearance of dry, flaky skin.
Leaves: Horsetail leaves are high in antioxidants, which can help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy, moisturized skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried equisetum arvense plants in the morning after dew has evaporated, ensuring at least 50% of plant material contains silica.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces, mixing equal parts of root and leaf for a balanced tincture with optimal mineral content.
Combine the chopped plant material with 60-70% vodka or other high-proof alcohol in a clean glass jar, ensuring the plant material is completely submerged for a ratio of 1:4 to 1:5.
Allow the mixture to steep for four weeks, shaking the jar daily, allowing the solvent to extract the bioactive compounds from the horsetail plant material.

Strain and filter the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter after four weeks, discarding the solids and transferring the clear liquid into dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tincture helps with dry skin because it contains properties that can moisturize and soothe the skin. It has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce redness and irritation, while also promoting the growth of new skin cells. Fennel is rich in vitamins and minerals, including vitamin C and E, that can help improve skin elasticity and reduce the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles. Additionally, fennel tincture can help improve circulation, which can promote the delivery of nutrients and oxygen to the skin, helping to keep it healthy and hydrated.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Fennel tincture's anti-inflammatory property, attributed to its volatile oils and flavonoids, helps reduce dry skin irritation and redness.
Moisturizing: The emollient properties of fennel tincture, derived from its essential oils and fatty acids, help lock in moisture and soothe dry skin.
Antioxidant: Fennel tincture's antioxidant effects, resulting from its flavonoids and phenolic acids, protect the skin from oxidative stress and dryness.
Exfoliating: The mild exfoliating property of fennel tincture, due to its alpha-hydroxy acids, aids in removing dead skin cells and improves dry skin texture.
Circulation-boosting: Fennel tincture's circulation-boosting property, attributed to its essential oils, promotes blood flow and helps nourish dry skin.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Fennel seeds are rich in fatty acids and antioxidants, making them ideal for soothing and moisturizing dry skin.
Leaves: The leaves of the fennel plant have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and irritation associated with dry skin.
Stems: Fennel stems contain high levels of vitamin C, which can help promote collagen production and improve skin elasticity in dry skin.
Flowers: Fennel flowers have antimicrobial properties, which can help keep dry skin free from infection and promote overall skin health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried foeniculum vulgare leaves and stems in late summer for optimal potency.
Clean and chop the harvested plant material into small pieces to increase surface area.
Combine 500 grams of chopped fennel with 1000 milliliters of high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for four weeks, shaking the jar daily, before straining and filtering the liquid.
Bottle the resulting tincture and store it in a cool dark place for up to two years after preparation.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat dry skin with herbal tinctures is by using a calendula tincture. This tincture, made from the calendula flower, has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe irritated skin. To use, mix a few drops of the tincture with a carrier oil like jojoba or almond oil, and gently massage into your skin. The tincture's healing properties will help to moisturize and rejuvenate dry, flaky skin. For best results, use twice daily.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to dry skin that are treated with herbal tinctures include eczema, psoriasis, and dermatitis. These conditions often involve dry, itchy, or inflamed skin. Herbal tinctures such as chamomile, calendula, and dandelion can help soothe and moisturize the skin, reducing irritation and inflammation. Additionally, these herbs have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties that promote skin healing and regeneration. It is essential to consult a healthcare professional before starting any new herbal treatments.
